title: Huoba Neva

{{Quote|Captain Neva's frigate is ''hit!''<s></s>|Leia Organa Solo, during the rescue mission to Coruscant|audio=LeiaNeva-DE.flac|<ref name="DE1" />}}
'''Huoba Neva''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]], and the daughter of a [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[Executive Board (SoroSuub Corporation)|Executive Board]] [[Huoba Neva's parent|member]]. Neva attended the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], graduating fourth in her class. However, her [[Non-huMan|status]] as a female non-[[Human/Legends|Human]] severely stunted her progression through the ranks. After several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of being passed over for promotion, and growing dissatisfied with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] treatment of non-Human [[Species/Legends|species]], she was contacted by [[Sian Tevv/Legends|Sian Tevv]]. A [[Politics/Legends|political]] agitator and member of the [[Sullustan Resistance|Sullustan resistance movement]], Tevv convinced Neva to defect to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

Neva found acceptance within the Alliance's ranks, and served both it and its successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], with dedication. The elimination of an Imperial-backed [[Illoud system insurgency|insurgency]] in the [[Illoud system/Legends|Illoud system]] earned her commendations and the command of the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Rebel Star]]'', making her the youngest Sullustan [[Warship/Legends|warship]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Navy]]. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Neva [[Death/Legends|died]] during a [[Second Battle of Coruscant|mission]] to rescue the crew of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Liberator'']] from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], which had been damaged by Imperial forces while on a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. As the ''Rebel Star'' emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], it collided with battle debris in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with the loss of all hands.

==Biography==
===An Imperial career===
Huoba Neva was the daughter of a [[Huoba Neva's parent|member]] of the [[Executive Board (SoroSuub Corporation)|Executive Board]] of the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]. She excelled in team games and sports, and her academic qualifications—along with her [[Parent/Legends|parent's]] prominent position within SoroSuub—earned her a place at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]]. Although accepting of SoroSuub's support, she wanted to get by on her own merits, seeing the Academy as a challenge and an opportunity to test her skills and capabilities. Being both non-[[Human/Legends|Human]] and [[Gender/Legends|female]], she knew that she would have a difficult time climbing the ranks of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], which was dominated by Human males. However, Neva—like all [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]]—had been taught that if one worked hard enough at achieving a goal, and deserved it, then that individual would be rewarded for their work. Neva was confident that if she applied herself, then she would attain her career targets.<ref name="DES" />

At the Academy, Neva majored in command school and weapons systems, studying with a fierce determination. She graduated fourth in her class, and was commissioned as an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the Navy. However, other [[Officer/Legends|officers]] regarded her as a "token [[Non-huMan|non-Human]]" within their ranks, disregarding her aptitude, skill, and training. Despite a spotless record and [[Politics/Legends|political]] reliability, Neva never attained her goal—the command of a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Initially, she had set her sights on commanding a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] ship or other combat vessel, but as the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] passed, she watched lesser officers promoted over her. Eventually, after a period of three years without a promotion, she realized that she had had enough. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] was not a meritocracy. Her experience with the Imperial Navy showed her that not all beings and organizations subscribed to the theory that her and her fellow Sullustans followed, and she began to wonder whether her—and SoroSuub's—commitment to the Empire was misplaced.<ref name="DES" />

===Defection and acceptance===
Neva's dissatisfaction with her career had not gone unnoticed; [[Sian Tevv/Legends|Sian Tevv]], a leading Sullustan [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] and high-ranking member of the [[Sullustan Resistance|Sullustan resistance movement]], had been watching her career with great interest. When it became apparent that her commitment to the Empire was faltering, he ordered his aides to approach Neva. They offered Neva the chance to defect and join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Neva was unsure at the beginning; she was not a revolutionary, and she had no real interest in politics. However, she was a [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], and she believed that if the Empire would not give her the chance to use her training and abilities to the fullest, then the Alliance would. She defected to the Alliance at the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], at a [[Time/Legends|time]] when SoroSuub was still a pro-Imperial company.<ref name="DES" />

Neva served the Alliance, and later, its successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],<ref name="DES" /> which was formed following the Alliance [[Battle of Endor/Legends|victory]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> She proved to be a capable naval [[Commander/Legends|commander]], as well as an accomplished [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]] [[operative]] and ground forces commander.<ref name="Rebellion" /> At some point, she led a mission to counter an Imperial-backed [[Illoud system insurgency|insurgency]] in the [[Illoud system/Legends|Illoud system]], an assignment that earned her several commendations and made her feel as if the transition from Empire to Republic had been truly worth it. She was subsequently promoted to command the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Rebel Star]]'', becoming the youngest Sullustan [[Warship/Legends|warship]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic fleet]].<ref name="DES" />

===Final mission===
{{Dialogue|Leia|<s></s>''Rebel Star'', ''Antares Six'', this is the ''Falcon''. There's too much debris here. Come around to zero-two. That's ''zero-two''. You'll find a gap in the wreckage.|Neva|Negative, ''Millennium Falcon''. We can make it, we can–|Officer|Watch it—Maximum thrust!! Hard left, hard left—|attr=Leia Organa Solo, Captain Huoba Neva, and an officer on the ''Rebel Star'' just prior to the frigate's destruction|audio=ManNeva-DE.flac|src=<ref name="DEA">[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], a resurgent Galactic Empire, led by Emperor Palpatine—who had been resurrected through the combination of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] technology<ref name="DE2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref>—[[Second Battle of Coruscant|took]] the New Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], forcing the Republic into retreat.<ref name="DE1" /> One of the Republic's [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]], the [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Liberator'']]—commanded by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—was assigned to [[Spy/Legends|spy]] on Imperial movements in the [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]]. During the mission, the vessel was caught in the middle of a battle between two Imperial forces, who had descended into [[Imperial Civil War|civil war]] following their conquest of Coruscant. The ''Liberator'' was badly damaged, and crashed onto the surface of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="DAOOC">{{WEGCite|story=Down And Out On Coruscant|book=Dark Empire Sourcebook}}</ref>

[[File:FirstAppearanceLastAppearance-DE1-1.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Rebel Star'' collides with wreckage, destroying the ship and killing Neva.]]
A rescue mission was assembled to extract the surviving crew; the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] captained by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and Captain [[Syub Snunb]]'s ''[[Antares Six]]'' joined Captain Neva's ''Rebel Star'' in attempting the mission.<ref name="DE1" /> As the small force sped through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the ''Falcon''{{'s}} [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] registered spatial turbulence at their re-entry coordinates, which was caused by a large amount of wrecked ships and battle debris from the recent conquest of Coruscant, and warned Neva and Snunb appropriately. Decamping from hyperspace, Solo realized that the density of wreckage would be too much for the [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] following him to handle; his wife, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], subsequently ordered the ships to change vector and go through a larger gap in the debris. Neva believed that the ''Rebel Star'' could make it through the wreckage behind the ''Falcon'', and stuck to her course. However, she had miscalculated.<ref name="DEA" /> The ''Rebel Star'' collided with the wreckage of a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], vanishing in a ball of flame. Neva was killed in the collision. Despite the loss of the ''Rebel Star'', the ''Falcon'' and ''Antares Six'' were able to complete their mission and rescue the surviving crew of the ''Liberator''.<ref name="DE1" />

==Personality and traits==
Huoba Neva was a perfectionist, and was determined to excel at whatever she chose to do. She firmly believed in the Sullustan view that if individuals worked hard enough, wanted something hard enough, and deserved to have it, then they would attain it. Despite being politically well-connected, Neva wanted to succeed—or even fail—based on her own merits and never relied on the favors of others. She had a strong connection to her family, and kept a [[Holocrystal/Legends|holocrystal]] of them with her at all times.<ref name="DES" />

Neva entered the Imperial Academy, viewing the military as a challenge, and aspired to become a distinguished officer with a command of her own. However, due to her status as a female non-Human, she was constantly passed over for promotion in favor of less-talented officers. The situation vexed her, calling into doubt her belief in her Sullustan outlook, until she joined the Rebel Alliance, and subsequently, the New Republic, despite having no prior taste for rebellion or politics. She reasoned that if the Empire refused to use her talents as a soldier, then the Alliance would. Neva's decision to defect was justified by her experiences within an organization that recognized and rewarded her skills and talents with a command of her own.<ref name="DES" />

A capable soldier, Neva was comfortable in command of both naval and ground units, and had experience as a special forces operative in addition to her training in starship command systems.<ref name="Rebellion" /> She was trained with both [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and blaster [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]], and in [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]]. Neva specialized in [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] tactics, and was able to pilot both space [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], and was trained in the use of their weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] systems. Her technical training included the programming and repair of [[Computer/Legends|computers]], the programming of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and the use of security systems. Neva was known to gamble, and she had knowledge of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], other cultures, [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]] and the workings of a bureaucracy.<ref name="DES" />

==Equipment==
Despite being an officer in the New Republic Defense Fleet, Houba Neva wore a Rebel Alliance uniform while on duty. She carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="DES" />

==Behind the scenes==
Huoba Neva was introduced and killed in the [[Dark Empire 1|first issue]] of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' in [[1991]], written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]].<ref name="DE1" /> The character was later expanded on in an entry in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]], which established her as a female Sullustan.<ref name="DES" /> In [[1994]], the character was also present in the opening scene of the [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of ''Dark Empire'', where Neva was given a masculine, Human-sounding voice.<ref name="DEA" />

Neva was also one of the Rebel Alliance characters featured in the video game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], receiving a mention in [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|the strategy guide for the game]]. Huoba Neva is referred to as a male in the latter publication. ''Rebellion'' is, to date, the only source that has physically depicted Neva.

In [[2001]], ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' included a list of character names that AI players could be assigned. Huoba Neva was included on the list, and an AI player using the Rebel Alliance could be randomly assigned the name.<ref name="SWGB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

In [[Sullustese/Legends|Sullustese]], "Huoba" is a male name meaning "powerful" and "Neva" means "legal council."<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>
